title: Multi-Account Position Aggregation
summary: Aggregate positions across multiple IBKR accounts into a single view — unified P&L, exposure, and risk metrics.
approach: Extend portfolio.py to iterate over account list from config, merge position dicts, compute net exposure per symbol.

title: Per-Account Risk Limits
summary: Enforce independent risk limits (max drawdown, position size, sector concentration) per account rather than globally.
approach: Refactor risk.py to accept account_id parameter, load per-account limits from config, gate orders per account.

title: Account-Aware Order Router
summary: Route orders to the correct IBKR account based on strategy assignment — each strategy maps to one or more accounts.
approach: Add strategy-to-account mapping in config, update orders.py to select account before placing. Handle account switching in IBKR API layer.

title: Cross-Account Correlation Guard
summary: Detect and flag when positions across accounts create unintended correlated exposure — e.g. long AAPL in two accounts doubles effective risk.
approach: Build correlation matrix across all account positions, alert when combined exposure exceeds threshold. Add to risk dashboard.

title: Unified Trade Journal
summary: Single trade journal that logs entries/exits across all accounts with account tag, strategy tag, and P&L attribution.
approach: Extend journal.py to accept account_id, add account column to trade log, update dashboard journal view to filter by account.

title: Account Performance Comparison
summary: Side-by-side performance comparison dashboard for all accounts — Sharpe, max drawdown, win rate, P&L curves per account and combined.
approach: New dashboard panel that pulls per-account equity curves from IBKR, computes metrics independently, renders comparison charts.

title: Order Fill Watcher — Execution Management Layer
summary: Background daemon thread that monitors a PENDING limit buy order after placement and chases price upward in configurable steps to secure a fill. Cancels if price ceiling or max attempts exhausted. Rule-based (no AI calls) for execution speed. 10 unit tests passing.
approach: New fill_watcher.py with FillWatcher class + stop_watcher() registry. Integrated into execute_buy() (start), execute_sell() (stop), _flatten_all_inner() (stop-all). Config block in config.py. All events logged to audit_log.jsonl.

title: Execution Agent — AI-Powered Order Execution Layer
summary: Replace rule-based fill watcher with a Claude-powered execution agent that reasons about market microstructure — spread, volume, VWAP, time of day — to decide order type, aggression, size splitting, and fill strategy per trade. Phase gate: must ship before live trading.
approach: New execution_agent.py. Agent receives trade signal + market microstructure data, returns structured execution plan {order_type, limit_price, aggression, split, timeout, fallback}. Fill watcher becomes one sub-strategy within the plan.
